insight: One thing that stood out to me was seeing leadership operate as a network rather than a ladder. During the morning critique, everyone had a voice regardless of their title or role. The culture felt highly collaborative, and it was clear that people felt ownership over their work. Seeing how much employees genuinely enjoyed working there showed me how trust and inclusion can positively impact morale.
goal: I want to continue building relationships with professionals in accessibility and design leadership. After connecting with Brian at Superhuman, my goal is to seek more opportunities for mentorship and feedback while continuing to strengthen my own accessibility-focused design practice.
different: In future group projects, I will make a more intentional effort to create space for everyone's voice to be heard. I want to practice a more collaborative leadership style that encourages participation and shared ownership rather than relying on a top to down laddering approach.

insight: I took this week to catch up on some of the readings and go back through my work thus far. I am really teetering to ping pong between tech and NGO's for next steps.  The leadership guest lectures have been so inspiring showing me that designers in tech are mostly still designing for good but I am starting to see how insanely tough it will be to break into as a junior or mid level during this current hiring climate. I have been reading between the lines and hearing a common thread repeated. It seems as though seniors and PM's have been tasked with more work as layoffs are happening to the junior designers and are being pushed to use more AI in their work. It makes sense from a business standpoint for sure, it's just pretty disheartening for someone like myself who is older, changing directions in their career, and hoping to break into something new that is definitely at a crossroads.
goal: I am setting new goals weekly for myself. ALways working on staying relevant in the design world by keeping up with new tools and working on my soft skills with interactions.
different: I am keeping an open mind here, and it can be quite challenging. I want to allow myself the time to create more sites, This class has really pushed me to do just that for all these exercises, and it can be daunting at the moment, but hey, I love being pushed and its is strengthening my AI prototyping skills, I have dropped the "Vibe Coding" term from my vocabulary.

week: Guest Speakers — Maggie Yue + Indi Young
insight: I really gelled with Indi Youngs chat, especially the examples of skyline, the gaps, the thinking styles.and the measurements and tracking of help and harm.
goal: I want to become more confident presenting my design work and explaining my process clearly without sounding overly scripted.
different: Before each presentation or critique, I’ll practice explaining the project out loud in a conversational way instead of memorizing a full script. I’ll focus on the problem, the design decision, and why it matters.

insight:
Working in Claude Code through the terminal has been a
  completely different experience than vibe coding on the
  desktop. Instead of clicking through a GUI and hoping
  something generates, I was describing what I wanted and
  watching it get written into actual code — files I could
  open, read, and own. Along the way I picked up things I
  didn't expect to: what a commit actually is, why pushing
  to GitHub doesn't automatically mean your site updates,
  how to debug a deployment failure, and why my diary
  entries live in the browser instead of on a server. The
  terminal felt intimidating at first but it turned out to
  be more direct — less guessing, more understanding. I'm
  not just leaving with a built thing. I'm leaving with a
  clearer mental model of how the web works.
goal: Keep pushing, or deploying I should say. I plan on learning more through trail and error and creating more with this new skill.
different: I will try to be less reliant upon the vibe coding desktop and create with what I am calling, UX/AI Prototyping now.

week: Guest Speakers — Eliel Johnson + James Song
insight: I learned how leadership is traditionally organized and additionally interesting new tactics of diving up how leadership and roles can be structured to benefit collaboration instead of just teams.
goal: I am pondering different goals currently, as this is to be used as a "Diary" I can say I am being ripped apart on my original aspirations and goals going into this program. This class just might be the unexpected deciding factor for my next adventure into a new profession. I may have squinted when looking forward for my walkabout into pursuing a career in tech.
different: my plan is still in full effect, to be bright-eyed and bushy-tailed when it comes to these new experiences. I hear so many conflicting perspectives about the future of tech and designers. I plan on staying hopeful and trudging forward.
